heading: Mergers and transfers between multiemployer plans
label: 1411 Mergers and transfers between multiemployer plans
notes: Effective Date of 2014 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–235, div. O, title I, § 121(b)128 Stat. 2794Dec. 16, 2014“The amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall apply with respect to plan years beginning after .”December 31, 2014
Effective DatePart effective , except as specifically provided, see .Sept. 26, 1980section 1461(e) of this title
amendments: Effective Date of 2014 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–235, div. O, title I, § 121(b)128 Stat. 2794Dec. 16, 2014“The amendments made by this section [amending this section] shall apply with respect to plan years beginning after .”December 31, 2014

children.heading: Authority of plan sponsor
children.label: a Authority of plan sponsor
children.text: Unless otherwise provided in regulations prescribed by the corporation, a plan sponsor may not cause a multiemployer plan to merge with one or more multiemployer plans, or engage in a transfer of assets and liabilities to or from another multiemployer plan, unless such merger or transfer satisfies the requirements of subsection (b).

in accordance with regulations of the corporation, the plan sponsor of a multiemployer plan notifies the corporation of a merger with or transfer of plan assets or liabilities to another multiemployer plan at least 120 days before the effective date of the merger or transfer;
no participant’s or beneficiary’s accrued benefit will be lower immediately after the effective date of the merger or transfer than the benefit immediately before that date;
the benefits of participants and beneficiaries are not reasonably expected to be subject to suspension under ; andsection 1426 of this title

an actuarial valuation of the assets and liabilities of each of the affected plans has been performed during the plan year preceding the effective date of the merger or transfer, based upon the most recent data available as of the day before the start of that plan year, or other valuation of such assets and liabilities performed under such standards and procedures as the corporation may prescribe by regulation.

children.heading: Actions not deemed violation of section 1106(a) or (b)(2) of this title
children.label: c Actions not deemed violation of section 1106(a) or (b)(2) of this title
children.text: The merger of multiemployer plans or the transfer of assets or liabilities between multiemployer plans, shall be deemed not to constitute a violation of the provisions of or if the corporation determines that the merger or transfer otherwise satisfies the requirements of this section.section 1106(a) of this titlesection 1106(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Nature of plan to which liabilities are transferred
children.label: d Nature of plan to which liabilities are transferred
children.text: A plan to which liabilities are transferred under this section is a successor plan for purposes of .section 1322a(b)(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: When requested to do so by the plan sponsors, the corporation may take such actions as it deems appropriate to promote and facilitate the merger of two or more multiemployer plans if it determines, after consultation with the Participant and Plan Sponsor Advocate selected under , that the transaction is in the interests of the participants and beneficiaries of at least one of the plans and is not reasonably expected to be adverse to the overall interests of the participants and beneficiaries of any of the plans. Such facilitation may include training, technical assistance, mediation, communication with stakeholders, and support with related requests to other government agencies.section 1304 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: one or more of the multiemployer plans participating in the merger is in critical and declining status (as defined in );section 1085(b)(4) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: such financial assistance will reduce the corporation’s expected long-term loss with respect to the plans involved; and

children.label: ii
children.text: such financial assistance is necessary for the merged plan to become or remain solvent;

children.label: C
children.text: the corporation certifies that its ability to meet existing financial assistance obligations to other plans will not be impaired by such financial assistance; and

children.label: D
children.text: such financial assistance is paid exclusively from the fund for basic benefits guaranteed for multiemployer plans.
